blocks.content: She typed it the way she texted Lena most things she couldn't quite say out loud — not in complete sentences, not with full context. Just the edges of it. The shape of the thing without the name.
Yeah. I was so close. And then I put it back.
A pause. She could picture Lena sitting up in her bed, the specific focus she got when something mattered.
I will be. I just can't explain the whole thing right now. My brain is too full.
Come in before my shift tomorrow. I'll have tea.
You don't have to have it figured out. Just come.
Goodnight, Cass. You're okay. You're doing okay.
She set the phone down. The room was still. The streetlight still made the same shape on the wall.
She closed her eyes and let the day go.
